problems.name: Systemic lupus erythematosus, active with inflammatory myositis (overlap PM/DM features)
problems.diagnosedBy: Initial 1/12/2026; refined to SLE-myositis with overlap PM/DM by Dr. Tanaka 3/22/2027 after muscle biopsy + MSA panel results
problems.notes: Disease has demonstrated full multi-system activity. Muscle biopsy (3/19/2027) shows active inflammatory myopathy with overlap PM/DM features (CD8+ T-cell endomysial infiltrate, perifascicular atrophy, diffuse MHC-I, C5b-9 perifascicular). MSA panel all negative — supports SLE-myositis over primary DM. Currently in remission on pulse-MP followed by prednisone 30 mg + MMF 1.5g BID + HCQ 200 BID. As of 4/22/2027 follow-up: dsDNA 16, complements C3 86/C4 11 (near-normal), CK 218 (near-normal), excellent treatment response.

problems.name: Oropharyngeal dysphagia (myositis-related) — resolving
problems.diagnosedBy: First reported 12/22/2026 by Dr. Williams (PCP); MBSS confirmed 2/9/2027 and 3/17/2027; substantial resolution by 4/15/2027 MBSS
problems.notes: Onset late 2026, severe at 3/15/2027 admission (silent aspiration on MBSS). Substantial recovery with treatment of underlying myositis: NG tube removed 4/15/2027 with safe full PO diet (chin-tuck precautions for thin liquids). Outpatient SLP ongoing.

problems.name: Inflammatory myositis (SLE-associated with overlap PM/DM features)
problems.notes: CK trajectory: 412 (12/2026) -> 638 (1/2027) -> 1840 (2/2027) -> 2580 peak (HD3 admission) -> 1820 (HD8) -> 1240 (discharge) -> 218 (4 weeks post-discharge). Aldolase trended similarly. Treatment: pulse methylprednisolone 1g x 3 then prednisone taper + MMF 1.5g BID + HCQ. Continues to improve.

problems.name: Severe protein-calorie malnutrition (kwashiorkor pattern) — recovering
problems.notes: Severe at admission (albumin 1.6, prealbumin 6, anasarca, kwashiorkor dermatosis, 'flag sign' hair). 4-week post-discharge: albumin 3.4, prealbumin 28 (normal), weight +9.4 kg from nadir. Skin findings substantially improved. Continued nutrition follow-up; goal to return to baseline weight (50.8 kg) over 4-6 months.

problems.name: Lupus nephritis (early; class undetermined) — resolved
problems.notes: UPC peaked at 0.62 g/d (HD7); now 0.15 (normal) at 4-week post-discharge. Renal biopsy deferred initially and now NOT indicated given resolution. Will continue periodic UPC monitoring.

problems.name: Alopecia, diffuse non-scarring — recovering
problems.notes: 'Flag sign' hair from nutritional depletion; new growth visible at hairline as of 4/22/2027 follow-up. Will improve over 6-12 months.

problems.name: Hashimoto's thyroiditis — controlled
problems.notes: Levothyroxine increased from 25 mcg to 50 mcg during 3/2027 admission. TSH 2.84 / free T4 1.2 on 4/22/2027 — well-controlled.

problems.name: Pancytopenia — resolved
problems.notes: Tri-lineage cytopenia at admission resolved by 4-week post-discharge. WBC 4.40, Hgb 10.8 (mild anemia of chronic inflammation remains), Plt 232.

problems.name: Depression, moderate — improving
problems.notes: PHQ-9 14 (admission) → 6 (4 weeks post-discharge — mild). Continues weekly Spanish-speaking therapy at Esperanza Behavioral Health.

problems.name: Unintentional weight loss / cachexia — recovering
problems.notes: Nadir 35.4 kg (BMI 15.7); 4 weeks post-discharge 44.8 kg (BMI 19.9). No longer underweight. Continued recovery expected.

problems.name: Loss of health insurance coverage — RESOLVED
problems.notes: Maryland Medicaid APPROVED 4/8/2027 (coverage through 12/31/2027). Will need annual renewal but currently has insurance for first time since 2020.

problems.name: Medication non-adherence due to access barriers — RESOLVED
problems.notes: Adherence cycle of 2026-2027 driven by insurance barriers. Now resolved with Medicaid coverage and Hopkins financial-aid integration; future adherence is the priority outcome.
